I agree that the proper course of action following treatment of a Hepatitis B-positive patient involves thorough sterilization using an autoclave. An additional point supporting this decision is the fact that autoclaving can achieve sporicidal activity, meaning it kills even highly resilient bacterial spores along with viruses like HBV. This makes it particularly effective in eliminating any residual infectious agents left behind by the patient’s condition.
